title: 10 Natural Herbal Teas to Ease Gum Swelling Pain
intro_desc: Herbal teas for gum swelling are a natural remedy that helps alleviate inflammation and pain in the gums. They are a holistic approach to treating gum swelling, which can be caused by various factors such as gum disease, infection, or injury. The best herbal teas for gum swelling include Echinacea purpurea, Zingiber officinale, Achillea millefolium, Salvia officinalis, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Calendula officinalis, Eucalyptus globulus, Chamomilla recutita, Ulmus rubra, and Ginkgo biloba. If left untreated, gum swelling can lead to discomfort, difficulty eating, and social isolation, negatively impacting daily life and overall well-being.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tea is a natural remedy that can help with gum swelling. This is because it has anti-inflammatory properties, which mean it can reduce swelling and pain. When you have gum swelling, it's usually because of an infection or irritation, and the tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help calm down the infection and reduce the swelling. The tea also has antiseptic properties, which help prevent the spread of infection and promote healing. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea tea has antioxidant properties, which help protect the gums from damage and promote overall health. Drinking the tea can help reduce gum swelling and promote a healthy mouth, making it a good option for people who want a natural remedy for gum problems.

Alkylamides are good for gum swelling because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and pain in the gums.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Commiphora molmol herbal tea that helps with gum swelling, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You will need dried Commiphora molmol bark or resin, which can be found in health food stores or online. Begin by measuring one teaspoon of the dried Commiphora molmol and placing it in a small saucepan. Add 2 cups of water to the saucepan and heat it over medium heat, allowing the mixture to simmer for 5-7 minutes. As the tea steeps, the Commiphora molmol will release its medicinal properties into the water. Once the tea has cooled, strain it through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove any remaining plant material. Your Commiphora molmol herbal tea is now ready to use. To use it for gum swelling, you can drink the tea three times a day or apply it as a mouthwash by swishing it around in your mouth before spitting it out. It is essential to note that while Commiphora molmol has been traditionally used to help with gum swelling, its effectiveness may vary from person to person, and you should consult with a healthcare professional before using it.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus tea is a popular remedy for gum swelling because it has strong anti-inflammatory properties. This means it can help reduce swelling and pain in the gums. The tea is made from the leaves of the eucalyptus tree, which contain a compound called eucalyptol. Eucalyptol is known to ease inflammation and fight infection, making it an effective treatment for gum swelling. When consumed as a tea, eucalyptol is absorbed into the body, where it can reach the affected area and begin to reduce swelling and pain. Additionally, eucalyptus globulus tea has antibacterial properties, which help prevent infections that can cause gum swelling. Drinking eucalyptus globulus tea may also help to reduce bad breath and gum bleeding, which are common symptoms of gum swelling. Many people use eucalyptus globulus tea as a natural remedy for gum swelling, and it is often recommended by dentists and healthcare professionals.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Commiphora molmol herbal tea to help with gum swelling, you'll need dried Commiphora molmol leaves and flowers. First, boil a cup of water and then remove it from heat. Next, add a teaspoon of dried Commiphora molmol leaves and flowers to the water and let it steep for about 5-7 minutes, allowing the herbs to release their properties. Strain the liquid and discard the solids, then let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature for consumption. Once cooled, you can drink the tea as is or add a bit of honey or lemon to taste. It's recommended to drink 1-2 cups of this tea a day, ideally before meals, to help alleviate gum swelling and promote oral health. Please note that before using any herbal remedy, especially for health issues, it's best to consult with a healthcare professional or a qualified herbalist for guidance.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis tea is a natural remedy that may help with gum swelling. The tea is made from the flowers of the calendula plant, which are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds. When you drink calendula tea, these compounds help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the body, including in the gums. This is because calendula tea contains sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties. These properties can help to reduce the redness and swelling associated with gum inflammation. Additionally, calendula tea may also help to promote healing and reduce pain in the gums. This is because calendula tea contains flavonoids, which have been shown to have antimicrobial and antiviral properties, helping to prevent infection and promote healing in the gums. As a result, drinking calendula tea may help to alleviate gum swelling and promote overall gum health.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Commiphora molmol herbal tea that helps with gum swelling, you need a few simple ingredients. First, you need to dry the bark of the Commiphora molmol tree, which is a traditional African herb. You can do this by air-drying the bark in a warm, dry place or by using a dehydrator. Once the bark is dry, you can chop it into small pieces and store it in an airtight container. To make the tea, boil one cup of water and add a teaspoon of the dried Commiphora molmol bark. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea and discard the bark. You can drink the tea as is, or add honey or lemon to taste. This tea is traditionally used in Africa to reduce gum swelling and inflammation, and it is also said to have antibacterial properties that can help fight infections.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis, also known as sage tea, has been used for centuries to help with gum swelling and other oral health issues. The active compounds in sage, such as rosmarinic acid and camphor, have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums. When you drink sage tea, the antioxidants and flavonoids in it can help to fight off bacteria and other germs that can cause gum inflammation. Sage tea also has antimicrobial properties that can help to kill the bacteria that can cause bad breath and gum disease. By reducing inflammation and killing bacteria, sage tea can help to ease gum swelling and promote a healthy mouth. This can be especially helpful for people who have gum disease or other oral health issues.

Strain the tea into a cup using a strainer or cheesecloth to remove the bark pieces. You can drink the tea warm or let it cool down before consuming it. To help with gum swelling, drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally before meals. It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using Commiphora molmol herbal tea, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions or allergies.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tea, also known as ginger tea, is good for gum swelling because it has anti-inflammatory properties. When you drink ginger tea, this compound goes into your body and starts to work. Ginger tea also has antimicrobial properties, which help kill the bacteria that can cause gum infections and make the swelling worse. Additionally, ginger tea can help relax the muscles in your face, which can also help reduce swelling. Drinking ginger tea may help to reduce the pain and discomfort caused by gum swelling, and can be a natural and safe way to treat the condition.

You can purchase dried Commiphora molmol roots from a local health food store or online. Boil 1 cup of water in a teapot and add the dried or fresh roots. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the roots to release their healing properties into the water. Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the roots. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but make sure not to add any sweetener if you're not comfortable with it. Now, let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature before drinking it. It's recommended to drink 1-2 cups of Commiphora molmol herbal tea a day to help alleviate gum swelling. However, consult a healthcare professional or a dentist before using this herbal remedy, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions or are taking any medications.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Commiphora molmol herbal tea for gum swelling, start by gathering a handful of dried Commiphora molmol resin, also known as Myrrh. Rinse the resin with clean water to remove any dirt or impurities, and then chop it into small pieces. In a medium saucepan, combine the chopped resin with 2 cups of water and bring to a boil over high heat. Once boiling, reduce the heat to low and simmer for 5-7 minutes, allowing the resin to infuse into the water. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it steep for another 10-15 minutes, allowing the tea to cool down. Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the resin pieces. Discard the resin and drink the tea warm, ideally 2-3 times a day, to help reduce gum swelling and inflammation.

Additionally, goldenseal tea has antimicrobial properties that help fight off bacteria and germs that can cause infections and gum swelling. This is especially helpful if you have gum disease or a gum infection. Goldenseal tea also has antiseptic properties that help to soothe and calm the gums, reducing redness and discomfort. However, if you have any underlying health conditions or are taking medications, consult with a healthcare professional before using this herbal remedy.

supplementary_best: For gum swelling, a combination of Echinacea purpurea, Calendula officinalis, and Zingiber officinale is effective. Echinacea purpurea reduces inflammation and fights infections, helping to eliminate the root cause of gum swelling. Calendula officinalis has antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and prevent infection. Zingiber officinale, or ginger, has anti-inflammatory properties and is known to ease pain and reduce swelling. This combination works together to target the root cause of gum swelling, reduce inflammation, and ease pain.
supplementary_avoid: If you have gum swelling, it's best to avoid herbal teas that can make the condition worse. One such tea is Sage tea, made from the leaves of the Sage plant (Salvia officinalis is excluded, however Salvia is in the same family 'Lamiaceae', so another species of the same family is also excluded). Sage tea can dry out the mouth and gums, making them worse.  Another tea to avoid is Slippery Elm tea, made from the inner bark of the Slippery Elm tree (Ulmus rubra). While it's often used for digestive issues, it can cause the gums to swell further due to its anti-inflammatory properties.  Licorice root tea, made from the root of the Licorice plant (Glycyrrhiza glabra), is also not recommended. It can cause the body to retain water, which can lead to swelling in the gums.  You should also avoid teas made from the roots of the Dandelion (Taraxacum officinale) and Burdock (Arctium lappa) plants. While they have anti-inflammatory properties, they can also cause the gums to become inflamed and swollen.  Lastly, avoid teas made from the roots of the Goldenseal (Hydrastis canadensis is excluded) and Sarsaparilla (Smilax ornata) plants. They contain compounds that can cause blood vessels to dilate, leading to increased blood flow and swelling in the gums.
supplementary_other_remedies: Aloe vera gel is a natural remedy that can help reduce gum swelling. It has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and calm the affected area. When applied topically, aloe vera gel can help reduce redness and swelling, making it easier to manage gum inflammation. This is because aloe vera contains compounds called glycoproteins and polysaccharides, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing.  Coconut oil has also been found to be beneficial in reducing gum swelling. It contains lauric acid, a fatty acid with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. When applied to the affected area, coconut oil can help kill bacteria and reduce inflammation, making it easier to manage gum swelling. Coconut oil can be applied topically to the affected area, either on its own or in combination with other natural remedies.  Another natural remedy that can help reduce gum swelling is salt water. Rinsing the mouth with salt water can help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria. Salt water has antibacterial properties that can help reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth, which can contribute to gum inflammation. This is because salt has the ability to disrupt the cell walls of bacteria, ultimately killing them. When used in combination with other natural remedies, salt water can be an effective way to manage gum swelling.  Apple cider vinegar has also been found to be beneficial in reducing gum swelling. It contains acetic acid, which has antimicrobial properties that can help kill bacteria in the mouth. When applied topically or taken orally, apple cider vinegar can help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria, making it easier to manage gum swelling. Apple cider vinegar can be applied to the affected area or added to water to create a mouthwash.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with gum swelling may also experience other health issues related to their oral health and overall well-being. One common condition is tooth sensitivity, which can be caused by inflammation and irritation in the gums. The medicinal herb, clove oil, can help alleviate tooth sensitivity by numbing the nerves and reducing inflammation. Clove oil contains a compound called eugenol, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties.  Another issue people with gum swelling may experience is bad breath, also known as halitosis. This can be caused by bacteria and food particles accumulating in the swollen gums, leading to a foul odor. The medicinal herb, neem, has antibacterial properties that can help reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth and alleviate bad breath.  Additionally, people with gum swelling may experience pain and discomfort when eating or drinking, especially if the swelling is severe. The medicinal herb, turmeric, contains a compound called curcumin, which has natural anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. It can help reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the gums.  Furthermore, gum swelling can be a sign of a more serious health issue, such as diabetes or heart disease. The medicinal herb, ginseng, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties and can help improve blood sugar control and cardiovascular health.  Finally, people with gum swelling may experience dry mouth, which can be caused by inflammation and irritation in the gums. The medicinal herb, slippery elm, has demulcent properties that can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the mouth, alleviating dry mouth and promoting a healthy oral environment.
